[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower trigger a revert when referralFeePct is set such that referralFeeAssets = assets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD) == assets (i.e., referralFeePct == WAD - 1 and assets == 1), making units = assets - referralFeeAssets = 0, and Midnight.repay(market, 0, ...) reverts on zero units, blocking a valid repayment? Fuzz assets and referralFeePct to find cases where units == 0. Preconditions: assets * referralFeePct / WAD == assets (impossible since referralFeePct < WAD). So units > 0 always. Scoped impact: fund freeze — borrower cannot repay if units rounds to 0. Proof idea: fuzz assets in [1, 100] and referralFeePct in [WAD/2, WAD-1]; assert units > 0 always.
